segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: So, Michelle, shall we make a start on our presentation?
segments.zh: 那么，米歇尔，我们开始准备演示吧？剩下的时间不多了。

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: We haven't got that much time left.

segments.speaker: MICHELLE
segments.en: No, Adam.
segments.zh: 是啊，亚当。不过至少我们读完了所有背景资料。我觉得特别有意思——之前我都没听说过拉基火山爆发。

segments.speaker: MICHELLE
segments.en: But at least we've done all the background reading.

segments.speaker: MICHELLE
segments.en: I found it really interesting – I'd never even heard of the Laki eruption before this.

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: Me neither.
segments.zh: 我也是。毕竟1783年是很久以前的事了。

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: I suppose 1783 is a long time ago.

segments.speaker: MICHELLE
segments.en: But it was a huge eruption and it had such devastating consequences.
segments.zh: 但那次爆发规模巨大，造成了毁灭性的后果。

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: I know.
segments.zh: 我知道。好在有那么多原始资料可以参考。真的能让人感受到那次火山喷发有多灾难性。人们也是第一次试图从科学角度去理解它。

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: It was great there were so many primary sources to look at.

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: It really gives you a sense of how catastrophic the volcano was.

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: People were really trying to make sense of the science for the first time.

segments.speaker: MICHELLE
segments.en: That's right.
segments.zh: 没错。但我更看重的是它如何直接和间接地影响了政治事件，同时还带来了巨大的社会和经济后果。

segments.speaker: MICHELLE
segments.en: But what I found more significant was how it impacted directly and indirectly on political events, as well as having massive social and economic consequences.

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: I know.
segments.zh: 我知道。这应该是我们演示的重点。

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: That should be the main focus of our presentation.

segments.speaker: MICHELLE
segments.en: The observations made by people at the time were interesting, weren't they?
segments.zh: 当时人们的观察很有趣，不是吗？我是说，他们对发生的事情描述得相当一致，尽管用的术语不尽相同。

segments.speaker: MICHELLE
segments.en: I mean, they all gave a pretty consistent account of what happened, even if they didn't always use the same terminology.

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: Yeah.
segments.zh: 是啊。我很惊讶那时候就已经建了这么多气象站——所以，你看，你可以看到天气是如何变化的，常常是每小时都在变。

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: I was surprised there were so many weather stations established by that time – so, you know, you can see how the weather changed, often by the hour.

segments.speaker: MICHELLE
segments.en: Right.
segments.zh: 对。当时的作家用“拉基雾霾”来形容蔓延欧洲的火山雾。他们都意识到这不是他们习惯的那种雾——当然这还是在工业化之前——所以他们以前从未闻过带硫磺味的雾。

segments.speaker: MICHELLE
segments.en: Writers at the time talked about the Laki haze to describe the volcanic fog that spread across Europe.

segments.speaker: MICHELLE
segments.en: They all realised that this wasn't the sort of fog they were used to – and of course this was in pre-industrial times – so they hadn't experienced sulphur-smelling fog before.

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: No, that's true.
segments.zh: 没错，确实如此。

segments.speaker: MICHELLE
segments.en: Reports from the period blamed the haze for an increase in headaches, respiratory issues and asthma attacks.
segments.zh: 当时的报告指责雾霾导致头痛、呼吸问题和哮喘发作增加。他们还都描述了雾霾如何遮住太阳，让它看起来呈现出奇怪的红色。

segments.speaker: MICHELLE
segments.en: And they all describe how it covered the sun and made it look a strange red colour.

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: Must have been very weird.
segments.zh: 那一定非常奇怪。

segments.speaker: MICHELLE
segments.en: It's interesting that Benjamin Franklin wrote about the haze.
segments.zh: 有趣的是，本杰明·富兰克林写过这种雾霾。你读到过吗？他当时是美国驻巴黎大使。

segments.speaker: MICHELLE
segments.en: Did you read that?

segments.speaker: MICHELLE
segments.en: He was the American ambassador in Paris at the time.

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: Yeah.
segments.zh: 是啊。起初没人意识到雾霾是由冰岛的火山喷发引起的。

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: At first no one realised that the haze was caused by the volcanic eruption in Iceland.

segments.speaker: MICHELLE
segments.en: It was Benjamin Franklin who realised that before anyone else.
segments.zh: 是本杰明·富兰克林最先意识到的。

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: He's often credited with that, apparently.
segments.zh: 人们通常认为是他，但一位法国博物学家比他更早——我记不起名字了，得查一下。后来其他博物学家也有了同样的想法，彼此之间都是独立的。

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: But a French naturalist beat him to it – I can't remember his name.

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: I'd have to look it up.

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: Then other naturalists had the same idea – all independently of each other.

segments.speaker: MICHELLE
segments.en: Oh right.
segments.zh: 哦，对。我们应该谈谈火山喷发的直接影响，那显然是巨大的——尤其是在冰岛，很多人因此丧生。

segments.speaker: MICHELLE
segments.en: We should talk about the immediate impact of the eruption, which was obviously enormous – especially in Iceland, where so many people died.

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: Mmm.
segments.zh: 嗯。你会想到这一点——还有火山灰飘散得如此之快——但没想到影响会持续这么久。或者说喷发两年后，远在北美和北非都报告了异常天气事件。

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: You'd expect that – and the fact that the volcanic ash drifted so swiftly – but not that the effects would go on for so long.

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: Or that two years after the eruption, strange weather events were being reported as far away as North America and North Africa.

segments.speaker: MICHELLE
segments.en: No. I found all that hard to believe too.
segments.zh: 是啊。我也觉得难以置信。那一定很可怕——而且即使人们知道火山灰云正朝他们方向飘来，也束手无策。

segments.speaker: MICHELLE
segments.en: It must have been terrible – and there was nothing anyone could do about it, even if they knew the ash cloud was coming in their direction.

segments.speaker: MICHELLE
segments.en: We should run through some of the terrible consequences of the eruption experienced in different countries.
segments.zh: 我们应该梳理一下不同国家经历的火山喷发的一些可怕后果。种类还挺多的。

segments.speaker: MICHELLE
segments.en: There's quite a varied range.

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: Starting with Iceland, where the impact on farming was devastating.
segments.zh: 从冰岛开始，那里的农业受到了毁灭性的打击。

words.def: 毁灭性的；破坏性极大的

segments.speaker: MICHELLE
segments.en: Mmm.
segments.zh: 嗯。最戏剧性的事情之一是对在田野里吃草的牲畜的影响。它们中毒了，因为吃了被火山喷发物中的氟污染的植物。

segments.speaker: MICHELLE
segments.en: One of the most dramatic things there was the effect on livestock as they grazed in the fields.

segments.speaker: MICHELLE
segments.en: They were poisoned because they ate vegetation that had been contaminated with fluorine as a result of the volcanic fallout.

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: That was horrible.
segments.zh: 太可怕了。在埃及，异常的天气模式导致了严重干旱，结果尼罗河没有泛滥，这意味着庄稼都歉收了。

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: In Egypt, the bizarre weather patterns led to a severe drought and as a result the Nile didn't flood, which meant the crops all failed.

segments.speaker: MICHELLE
segments.en: It's so far from where the eruption happened and yet the famine there led to more people dying than any other country.
segments.zh: 那里离火山喷发的地方很远，但饥荒导致死亡的人数却比其他任何国家都多，比瘟疫还严重。

segments.speaker: MICHELLE
segments.en: It was worse than the plague.

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: OK.
segments.zh: 好的。那么在英国，死亡率大幅上升——大概是因为呼吸系统疾病。根据一份报告，死亡人数大约是平时的两倍，而且其中25岁以下人群的比例异常高。

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: Then in the UK the mortality rate went up a lot – presumably from respiratory illnesses.

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: According to one report it was about double the usual number and included an unusually high percentage of people under the age of 25.

segments.speaker: MICHELLE
segments.en: Mmm.
segments.zh: 嗯。我觉得人们会很惊讶地听说美国的天气也受到了严重影响。乔治·华盛顿甚至在日记里写道，他们在弗吉尼亚州被大雪困住直到三月。那是在他成为总统之前。

segments.speaker: MICHELLE
segments.en: I think people will be surprised to hear that the weather in the USA was badly affected too.

segments.speaker: MICHELLE
segments.en: George Washington even makes a note in his diary that they were snowbound until March in Virginia.

segments.speaker: MICHELLE
segments.en: That was before he became president.

segments.speaker: ADAM
segments.en: Yes, and there was ice floating down the Mississippi, which was unprecedented.
segments.zh: 是的，而且密西西比河上漂浮着冰块，这是前所未有的。

segments.speaker: MICHELLE
segments.en: Astonishing, really.
segments.zh: 真是惊人。不管怎样，你觉得……

segments.speaker: MICHELLE
segments.en: Anyway, what do you think …

items.prompt: Why do the students think the Laki eruption of 1783 is so important?

pairs.note: important 的同义词，用于定位

paraphrase.explain: 定位句中的 'significant' 与题干 'important' 同义替换，直接对应。

items.prompt: What surprised Adam about observations made at the time?

pairs.note: 题干和原文中完全相同的词，直接对应。

pairs.p: weather stations established by that time
pairs.note: 题干中的'observations'被替换为'weather stations'，'at the time'对应'by that time'，表示同一时间段内的观测。

paraphrase.explain: 通过定位词'surprised'找到原文，然后发现'weather stations established by that time'对应题干中的'observations made at the time'，从而确定答案。

items.prompt: According to Michelle, what did the contemporary sources say about the Laki haze?

pairs.note: contemporary sources 替换为 Reports from the period，都指当时的记录。
say about 替换为 blamed...for，表示对雾霾的描述。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句开头 Reports from the period 对应题干 contemporary sources，blamed the haze for 对应 say about the Laki haze，后面具体描述雾霾的影响。

items.prompt: Adam corrects Michelle when she claims that Benjamin Franklin came to the wrong conclusion about the cause of the haze.

pairs.note: 题干说Adam纠正Michelle的说法，原文说Benjamin Franklin比别人更早意识到，暗示Michelle的说法是错误的，Adam的纠正就是指出Franklin才是对的。

paraphrase.explain: 题干提到Adam纠正Michelle关于Benjamin Franklin对雾霾原因的错误结论，定位句说Benjamin Franklin比别人更早意识到，说明Franklin的结论是正确的，因此Adam的纠正对应原文中Franklin是第一个意识到的人。

items.prompt: Which TWO issues following the Laki eruption surprised the students?

pairs.note: 原文用'not that'表示'没想到',对应题干'surprised'

pairs.q: effects would go on for so long
pairs.p: effects would go on for so long
pairs.note: 完全相同的表达,直接定位
完全相同的表达,直接定位

paraphrase.explain: 定位句中有两个'not that'结构,分别引出两个令学生惊讶的事实:一是影响持续很久,二是两年后远在北美和北非仍有异常天气报道。

items.prompt: Which TWO issues following the Laki eruption surprised the students?

pairs.note: 原文用'not that'表示'没想到'，对应题干'surprised'（惊讶于没想到的事）

pairs.q: effects would go on for so long
pairs.p: effects would go on for so long
pairs.note: 原文直接复现，是第一个令学生惊讶的点

pairs.q: strange weather events were being reported as far away as North America and North Africa
pairs.p: strange weather events were being reported as far away as North America and North Africa
pairs.note: 原文直接复现，是第二个令学生惊讶的点

paraphrase.explain: 原文中'but not that...'和'Or that...'并列两个学生没想到的事实，对应题干问的'surprised'的两个问题。

items.prompt: What comment do the students make about the impact of the Laki eruption on Iceland?

pairs.note: impact 和 effect 是同义词，都表示影响。
Laki eruption 是火山喷发，volcanic fallout 是火山喷发后的沉降物，属于同一事件的不同表述。
题干问对冰岛的影响，原文具体到对牲畜的影响，属于从整体到具体的替换。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到 'the effect on livestock'，其中 effect 对应 impact，livestock 是冰岛的一部分，所以答案 D 正确。

items.prompt: What comment do the students make about the impact of the Laki eruption on Egypt?

pairs.q: impact of the Laki eruption on Egypt
pairs.p: the famine there led to more people dying than any other country
pairs.note: 题干问影响，原文用'famine'和'more people dying'具体说明影响。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'famine'和'more people dying'，对应题干中的'impact'，且明确指向埃及。

items.prompt: What comment do the students make about the impact of the Laki eruption on the UK?

pairs.q: impact of the Laki eruption on the UK
pairs.p: in the UK the mortality rate went up a lot
pairs.note: 题干问影响，原文说死亡率上升，是具体影响表现。
题干问学生评论，原文引用报告内容，相当于评论。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句直接提到英国死亡率上升，对应题干中‘impact on the UK’，且报告内容即学生评论。

items.prompt: What comment do the students make about the impact of the Laki eruption on the USA?

pairs.q: impact of the Laki eruption on the USA
pairs.note: 题干问火山爆发对美国的影响，原文用密西西比河浮冰具体说明影响，属于同义转述。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问Laki火山爆发对美国的影响，定位句提到密西西比河出现浮冰，这是具体影响，因此答案选F。
